detail.answer: Gujarat had the highest installed wind capacity in 2025, with 13 450.120 MW total [6].

The top three states by installed wind power capacity in India as on 31.03.2025 are:  
1. Gujarat – 12 677.48 MW  
2. Tamil Nadu – 11 739.91 MW  
3. Karnataka – 7 351.10 MW [9]
India’s total installed wind power capacity as on 31 March 2025 was 50,038 MW. [1]
There were exactly 14 wind turbine generator (WEG) manufacturers offering WEGs in India as of 10 July 2025. [1]
GE contributed 929 MW of new wind power capacity in India during FY 2024-25. [1]
The largest wind‐energy generator rating installed in India is 5,200 kW. [1]
NIWE has installed 996 wind monitoring masts across India’s windy states. [1]
Electricity generation from windpower projects in India during FY 2024-25 was 83,347 Million Units (MU). [1][2]
By March 2025, non-fossil sources totaled 220 GW out of India’s 470 GW of installed capacity, i.e. just over 46 % [5].
India has realised 23.26 % of its estimated wind-power potential at 150 m above ground level as on 31 March 2025. [1]

The “State & Make-wise WEG Installations” table does list Senvion (SI No. 10) but its Madhya Pradesh columns are blank (no “No.” or “MW” shown) – i.e. no Senvion turbines are recorded for Madhya Pradesh in that table [3].
Senvion has installed 212 wind turbines in Gujarat [8].
Suzlon installed 147 wind turbines (WEGs) with a total capacity of 336.200 MW during FY 2024-25 in India. [2]

The provided directory gives only the national total of 996 NIWE wind‐monitoring masts and explicitly states that it “does not provide a state‐by‐state breakdown” [1]. No separate count for Tamil Nadu is available in these passages.
Suzlon installed 847 wind turbines in FY 2016-17 in India. [1]
Suzlon installed 435 wind turbines in FY 2015-16 [3]. The source does not provide any state-wise breakdown.
Siemense Gamesa installed 281 WEGs in FY 2020-21. [2]
Pioneer Wincon had installed 682 wind turbines up to March 2010 cumulatively. [2]
Inox Wind Ltd has installed more than 1,600 wind turbines across India [10].
GE added 929 MW of new wind-power capacity in India during FY 2024-25. [1]
